title: Why Upstart Shares Are Tumbling After AI Concerns
text: Model 22 Performance: Model 22 leveraged advanced neural networks to improve loan conversion rates and underwriting accuracy. Initially, the model showed impressive results: Model 22 helped drive conversion rates from 19% in Q1 to 24% in Q2, and the company reported $257 million in revenue, a 102% year-over-year increase, and achieved GAAP net income of $5.6 million in Q2 2025. Later Problems: However, the model encountered significant issues. The model overreacted to negative macroeconomic signals, became too conservative, and reduced borrower approvals and conversion rates. Upstart reported Q3 2025 revenue of $277 million, missing its own guidance of $280 million, and issued Q4 2025 revenue guidance of just $288 million versus the $303.7 million consensus. Stock Impact: Upstart shares slid after investors reacted to a sharp earnings miss and a wider-than-expected net loss. Upstart shares fell $4.49, or 9.71%, closing at $41.75 on November 5, 2025. Legal Consequences: A complaint was filed April 7, 2026, in the U.S. District Court alleging that Upstart and four of its top executives made materially false and misleading statements about the performance of Model 22 throughout a class period spanning May 14, 2025, to November 4, 2025.
